mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 540915M as of 2006-06-15 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings risk.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: When UNRATE trigger condition is met, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE change and 5-day returns exceeds -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) jointly determine real monetary conditions. When FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL growth rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real rates are restrictive, compressing equity valuations and producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 262.231 as of 2006-07-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with positive real rates
failure_condition: When real rate spread exceeds 1.5pp, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below its 6-month moving average, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO falls below 6-month MA, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO deceleration and 60-day returns exceeds -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture broad-based demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 20 days due to multiplier effects on corporate earnings and consumer confidence.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both positive MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1669K and RSAFS at 540915M as of 2006-06-15
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS show positive MoM growth, 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 45% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K per month, signaling labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending power and equity earnings expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth falls below 100K, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day returns exceeds -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over a 2-month window signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates on future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days without requiring threshold activation.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS decline and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or declining FEDFUNDS periods produce positive returns in < 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly determine aggregate demand momentum. When both series show positive growth rates simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to earnings growth expectations, producing positive 20-day forward returns in mid-cycle expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2% and PAYEMS MoM growth > 50K
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18780.325B and PAYEMS at 142631K as of 2006-04-15 and 2006-07-15
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized GDP and employment growth
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 and PAYEMS growth thresholds are met, 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 45% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
